align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun'''|亀ヶ森・鎮守森古墳|Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun}} are a pair of early [[Kofun period]] (late 4th century AD) [[kofun|megalithic tumuli]] located in what is now part of the town of [[:en:Aizubange, Fukushima|Aizubange, Fukushima]] in the southern [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/204843 |title=亀ヶ森・鎮守森古墳|trans-title= Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamegamori ''kofun'' is located in the northern part of Aizu on the west side of Aozu hamlet south of the [[:en:Aga River|Aga River]], in a rural area surrounded by rice fields. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a total length of 129.4 meters, with a rectangular portion length of 55.0 meters, and a rear circle diameter of 74.4 meters. It was built in three tiers with a horseshoe-shaped moat and dates from the latter portion of the Kofun period (the second half of the 4th century). It is the second largest keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' in the Tōhoku region after the [[Raijinyama Kofun]] in [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]]. The tumulus has not been fully excavated, but in a trial survey in 1992, fragments of cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' and ''[[fukiishi]]'' were uncovered. The mounds are covered in a dense growth of [[:en:Cryptomeria|Cryptomeria]] and on top of the circular portion of the tumulus is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] and a Buddhist chapel.
The Chinjumori ''kofun'' in orientated in the same southerly direction, approximately 100 meets to the south of the Kamegamori ''kofun''. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} with the form of "two conjoined rectangles", and has a length of 55.2 meters, with a 26.2-meter rectangular portion, five meters height, and an 18-meter rear portion, about 2.5 meters high. A portion of a surrounding moat has been found. It was partly excavated from 1990 to 1991, and is believed to date from slightly earlier than the Kamegamori ''kofun''. It also has a small Shinto shrine on its summit.
The site is approximately 20 minutes by car from [[:en:Aizu-Bange Station|Aizu-Bange Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Tadami Line|Tadami Line]]. At present, there are no public facilities at the site.

The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/204843 |title=亀ヶ森・鎮守森古墳|trans-title= Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamegamori ''kofun'' is located in the northern part of Aizu on the west side of Aozu hamlet south of the [[:en:Aga River|Aga River]], in a rural area surrounded by rice fields. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a total length of 129.4 meters, with a rectangular portion length of 55.0 meters, and a rear circle diameter of 74.4 meters. It was built in three tiers with a horseshoe-shaped moat and dates from the latter portion of the Kofun period (the second half of the 4th century). It is the second largest keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' in the Tōhoku region after the [[Raijinyama Kofun]] in [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]]. The tumulus has not been fully excavated, but in a trial survey in 1992, fragments of cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' and ''[[fukiishi]]'' were uncovered. The mounds are covered in a dense growth of [[:en:Cryptomeria|Cryptomeria]] and on top of the circular portion of the tumulus is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] and a Buddhist chapel.
The Chinjumori ''kofun'' in orientated in the same southerly direction, approximately 100 meets to the south of the Kamegamori ''kofun''. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} with the form of "two conjoined rectangles", and has a length of 55.2 meters, with a 26.2-meter rectangular portion, five meters height, and an 18-meter rear portion, about 2.5 meters high. A portion of a surrounding moat has been found. It was partly excavated from 1990 to 1991, and is believed to date from slightly earlier than the Kamegamori ''kofun''. It also has a small Shinto shrine on its summit.
The site is approximately 20 minutes by car from [[:en:Aizu-Bange Station|Aizu-Bange Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Tadami Line|Tadami Line]]. At present, there are no public facilities at the site.

The tumulus is a {{nihongo|{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} with the form of "two conjoined rectangles", and has a length of 55.2 meters, with a 26.2-meter rectangular portion, five meters height, and an 18-meter rear portion, about 2.5 meters high. pair of early [[Kofun period]] (late 4th century AD) [[kofun|megalithic tumuli]] located in what is now part of the town of [[:en:Aizubange, Fukushima|Aizubange, Fukushima]] in the southern {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/204843 |title=亀ヶ森・鎮守森古墳|trans-title= Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>

The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The mounds are covered in a dense growth of {{ill|Cryptomeria|en|Cryptomeria|WD=Q147388|lt=Cryptomeria}} and on top of the circular portion of the tumulus is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} and a Buddhist chapel.
The Chinjumori ''kofun'' in orientated in the same southerly direction, approximately 100 meets to the south of the Kamegamori ''kofun''. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} with the form of "two conjoined rectangles", and has a length of 55.2 meters, with a 26.2-meter rectangular portion, five meters height, and an 18-meter rear portion, about 2.5 meters high. A portion of a surrounding moat has been found. It was partly excavated from 1990 to 1991, and is believed to date from slightly earlier than the Kamegamori ''kofun''. It also has a small Shinto shrine on its summit.
The site is approximately 20 minutes by car from {{ill|Aizu-Bange Station|en|Aizu-Bange Station|WD=Q4699441|lt=Aizu-Bange Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Tadami Line|en|Tadami Line|WD=Q1054717|lt=Tadami Line}}. <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun]][[ja:亀ヶ森・鎮守森古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun]][[ja:亀ヶ森・鎮守森古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun]][[ja:亀ヶ森・鎮守森古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun]][[ja:亀ヶ森・鎮守森古墳]]
{{Short description|Ancient grave site in Fukushima, Tōhoku, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun | native_name = 亀ヶ森・鎮守森古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Kamegamori-kofun and Chinjumori-kofun.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kamegamori-kofun to the right and Chinjumori-kofun to the left | map_type = Japan Fukushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|37.593025|N|139.829103|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Aizubange|WD=Q1204988}} | region = {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region}} | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun'''|亀ヶ森・鎮守森古墳|Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun}} are a pair of early [[Kofun period]] (late 4th century AD) [[kofun|megalithic tumuli]] located in what is now part of the town of [[:en:Aizubange, Fukushima|Aizubange, Fukushima]] in the southern {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/204843 |title=亀ヶ森・鎮守森古墳|trans-title= Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamegamori ''kofun'' is located in the northern part of Aizu on the west side of Aozu hamlet south of the [[:en:Aga River|Aga River]], in a rural area surrounded by rice fields. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a total length of 129.4 meters, with a rectangular portion length of 55.0 meters, and a rear circle diameter of 74.4 meters. It was built in three tiers with a horseshoe-shaped moat and dates from the latter portion of the Kofun period (the second half of the 4th century). It is the second largest keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' in the Tōhoku region after the [[Raijinyama Kofun]] in {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|WD=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture}}. The tumulus has not been fully excavated, but in a trial survey in 1992, fragments of cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' and ''[[fukiishi]]'' were uncovered. The mounds are covered in a dense growth of {{ill|Cryptomeria|en|Cryptomeria|WD=Q147388|lt=Cryptomeria}} and on top of the circular portion of the tumulus is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} and a Buddhist chapel.
The Chinjumori ''kofun'' in orientated in the same southerly direction, approximately 100 meets to the south of the Kamegamori ''kofun''. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} with the form of "two conjoined rectangles", and has a length of 55.2 meters, with a 26.2-meter rectangular portion, five meters height, and an 18-meter rear portion, about 2.5 meters high. A portion of a surrounding moat has been found. It was partly excavated from 1990 to 1991, and is believed to date from slightly earlier than the Kamegamori ''kofun''. It also has a small Shinto shrine on its summit.
The site is approximately 20 minutes by car from {{ill|Aizu-Bange Station|en|Aizu-Bange Station|WD=Q4699441|lt=Aizu-Bange Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Tadami Line|en|Tadami Line|WD=Q1054717|lt=Tadami Line}}. At present, there are no public facilities at the site.
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)|WD=Q6573260|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== :{{Commons category-inline|Chinjumori Kofun}} *[https://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/278.html Aizubange town home page] {{in lang|ja}}

The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a total length of 129.4 meters, with a rectangular portion length of 55.0 meters, and a rear circle diameter of 74.4 meters. It was built in three tiers with a horseshoe-shaped moat and dates from the latter portion of the Kofun period (the second half of the 4th century). It is the second largest keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' in the Tōhoku region after the [[Raijinyama Kofun]] in {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|WD=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}. The tumulus has not been fully excavated, but in a trial survey in 1992, fragments of cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' and ''[[fukiishi]]'' were uncovered. The mounds are covered in a dense growth of {{ill|Cryptomeria|en|Cryptomeria|WD=Q147388|lt=Cryptomeria|1=Cryptomeria}} and on top of the circular portion of the tumulus is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} and a Buddhist chapel.
The Chinjumori ''kofun'' in orientated in the same southerly direction, approximately 100 meets to the south of the Kamegamori ''kofun''. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} with the form of "two conjoined rectangles", and has a length of 55.2 meters, with a 26.2-meter rectangular portion, five meters height, and an 18-meter rear portion, about 2.5 meters high. A portion of a surrounding moat has been found. It was partly excavated from 1990 to 1991, and is believed to date from slightly earlier than the Kamegamori ''kofun''. It also has a small Shinto shrine on its summit.
The site is approximately 20 minutes by car from {{ill|Aizu-Bange Station|en|Aizu-Bange Station|WD=Q4699441|lt=Aizu-Bange Station|1=Aizu-Bange Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Tadami Line|en|Tadami Line|WD=Q1054717|lt=Tadami Line|1=Tadami Line}}. At present, there are no public facilities at the site.

The mounds are covered in a dense growth of {{ill|Cryptomeria|en|Cryptomeria|qid=Q147388|lt=Cryptomeria|1=Cryptomeria}} and on top of the circular portion of the tumulus is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} and a Buddhist chapel.

The tumulus is a {{nihongo|{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} with the form of "two conjoined rectangles", and has a length of 55.2 meters, with a 26.2-meter rectangular portion, five meters height, and an 18-meter rear portion, about 2.5 meters high. A portion of a surrounding moat has been found. It was partly excavated from 1990 to 1991, and is believed to date from slightly earlier than the Kamegamori ''kofun''. It also has a small Shinto shrine on its summit.
The site is approximately 20 minutes by car from {{ill|Aizu-Bange Station|en|Aizu-Bange Station|qid=Q4699441|lt=Aizu-Bange Station|1=Aizu-Bange Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|qid=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Tadami Line|en|Tadami Line|qid=Q1054717|lt=Tadami Line|1=Tadami Line}}. At present, there are no public facilities at the site.
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)|qid=Q6573260|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)}}

{{Short description|Ancient grave site in Fukushima, Tōhoku, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] | name = Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun | native_name = 亀ヶ森・鎮守森古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Kamegamori-kofun and Chinjumori-kofun.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kamegamori-kofun to the right and Chinjumori-kofun to the left | map_type = Japan Fukushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|37.593025|N|139.829103|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Aizubange|qid=Q1204988|1=Aizubange}} | region = {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|qid=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region|1=Tōhoku region}} | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun'''|亀ヶ森・鎮守森古墳|Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun}} are a pair of early [[Kofun period]] (late 4th century AD) [[kofun|megalithic tumuli]] located in what is now part of the town of [[:en:Aizubange, Fukushima|Aizubange, Fukushima]] in the southern {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|qid=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region|1=Tōhoku region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/204843 |title=亀ヶ森・鎮守森古墳|trans-title= Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamegamori ''kofun'' is located in the northern part of Aizu on the west side of Aozu hamlet south of the [[:en:Aga River|Aga River]], in a rural area surrounded by rice fields. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpō-kōen-fun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=[[Zenpō-kōen-fun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a total length of 129.4 meters, with a rectangular portion length of 55.0 meters, and a rear circle diameter of 74.4 meters. It was built in three tiers with a horseshoe-shaped moat and dates from the latter portion of the Kofun period (the second half of the 4th century). It is the second largest keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' in the Tōhoku region after the [[Raijinyama Kofun]] in {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|qid=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}. The tumulus has not been fully excavated, but in a trial survey in 1992, fragments of cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' and ''[[fukiishi]]'' were uncovered. The mounds are covered in a dense growth of {{ill|Cryptomeria|en|Cryptomeria|qid=Q147388|lt=Cryptomeria|1=Cryptomeria}} and on top of the circular portion of the tumulus is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} and a Buddhist chapel.
The Chinjumori ''kofun'' in orientated in the same southerly direction, approximately 100 meets to the south of the Kamegamori ''kofun''. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} with the form of "two conjoined rectangles", and has a length of 55.2 meters, with a 26.2-meter rectangular portion, five meters height, and an 18-meter rear portion, about 2.5 meters high. A portion of a surrounding moat has been found. It was partly excavated from 1990 to 1991, and is believed to date from slightly earlier than the Kamegamori ''kofun''. It also has a small Shinto shrine on its summit.
The site is approximately 20 minutes by car from {{ill|Aizu-Bange Station|en|Aizu-Bange Station|qid=Q4699441|lt=Aizu-Bange Station|1=Aizu-Bange Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|qid=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Tadami Line|en|Tadami Line|qid=Q1054717|lt=Tadami Line|1=Tadami Line}}. At present, there are no public facilities at the site.
